surface.name: Build and view the Jira Forge hello-world issue panel
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Build and view the Jira Forge hello-world issue panel. The shared Forge setup links peer host-product tutorials, and the current Jira tutorial provides an atomic create, edit, deploy, install, and visible issue-panel path. Prior surface before policy commit: Atlassian product and Forge hello-world target not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Jira Forge hello world
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Jira is one of four coequal product tutorials offered after Forge setup.

alternatives_considered.name: Confluence Forge hello world
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Confluence has a distinct module, installation target, and UI verification path.

alternatives_considered.name: Bitbucket Forge hello world
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Bitbucket is a separate Atlassian product route, and older Connect material also exists even though Atlassian recommends Forge.

alternatives_considered.name: Jira Service Management Forge hello world
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This product-specific tutorial is not selected over the other Forge targets.

entry_point.developer_intent: Build and visibly verify a first Atlassian cloud app without a preselected Atlassian product.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Bitbucket, Confluence, Jira, or Jira Service Management app surface has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Jira Forge hello-world tutorial ends when the deployed and installed app panel is visible on a Jira issue with its configured title and hello-world content.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The app panel is visible on the Jira issue with the configured title and hello-world content.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The app panel is visible on the Jira issue with the configured title and hello-world content.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The app panel is visible on the Jira issue with the configured title and hello-world content.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Forge apps use JavaScript and the shared guide expects JavaScript familiarity.
Install Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x and run `node --version` before installing Forge CLI.
Install Forge CLI globally with npm and verify `forge --version` prints a version.
An Atlassian account, scoped Forge API token, and a free cloud developer site containing the selected product are required.

primary_path.action: Complete the shared Forge setup: install Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x and verify it, install Forge CLI and verify it, create a scoped Forge API token, authenticate, and create or finish a cloud developer site.
details: On macOS, Atlassian recommends nvm for Node installation. Installer permission errors may require correcting the Node installation, and a terminal restart can be needed before new commands resolve.
Token creation requires name, expiry, Forge app selection, and recommended or custom scopes.
`forge login` asks about analytics; keychain-less environments use `FORGE_EMAIL` and `FORGE_API_TOKEN` instead.
The site setup wizard must finish after provisioning.
primary_path.input: JavaScript familiarity, Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x, npm, Atlassian account, and email-associated developer site.
primary_path.output: Authenticated Forge CLI and cloud developer site.
primary_path.success_signal: Forge CLI can run authenticated commands and the developer site is ready.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A failed CLI install must be retried; site provisioning and setup can wait; missing libsecret requires environment-variable authentication; Forge must not run as root.

primary_path.action: Choose Bitbucket, Confluence, Jira, or Jira Service Management and enter that product's linked hello-world tutorial.
details: The common create, deploy, and install lifecycle begins with `forge create`, `forge deploy`, and `forge install`.
Template prompts, host product, visible app location, and terminal UI differ by tutorial.
primary_path.input: Human-selected Atlassian host product and a site containing it.
primary_path.success_signal: No common visible app signal exists until the product route is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without this choice, template, permissions, installation prompts, and verification pages cannot be normalized.

primary_path.action: Install Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x and run `node --version`.
details: On macOS, Atlassian recommends nvm; a terminal restart can be required before the command resolves.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unsupported version, install permission error, or command-not-found state requires correcting Node installation and retrying.

details: Forge must not be installed or run with root privileges.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If no version appears, repeat installation, inspect errors, repair npm permissions, and restart the terminal if needed.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to the Atlassian account and create a scoped Forge API token with name, expiry, Forge app selection, and recommended or custom scopes.
details: The account email and token authenticate Forge CLI.
primary_path.success_signal: The full token is available for CLI login.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing account access, token value, expiry, app selection, or scopes prevents authentication.

primary_path.action: Run `forge login`, answer the analytics prompt, and authenticate with the account email and scoped token.
details: On a system without supported keychain or libsecret, set `FORGE_EMAIL` and `FORGE_API_TOKEN` instead of running `forge login`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong token, scopes, email, keychain, or root execution requires correcting authentication and retrying.

primary_path.action: Create the free cloud developer site with the account email, wait for it to be ready, sign in, and complete the setup wizard.
details: The site must contain Jira and the testing user must be signed in because Forge apps are not visible anonymously.
primary_path.success_signal: The site setup wizard is complete and Jira opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Site provisioning or an incomplete wizard delays app installation.

primary_path.action: Run `forge create`, name the app, and select `development` when Forge prompts for the default environment.
details: The tutorial selects the default environment before the product, category, and template prompts.
primary_path.input: Authenticated Forge CLI and writable parent directory.
primary_path.output: Named Forge app creation flow with development as its default environment.
primary_path.success_signal: Forge advances to the Atlassian app selection after accepting `development`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Naming, environment selection, filesystem, authentication, or CLI errors prevent project creation.

primary_path.action: Select Jira as the Atlassian app, select UI Kit as the category, select `jira-issue-panel` as the template, and enter the generated app subdirectory.
primary_path.input: Forge create flow configured for the development environment.
primary_path.success_signal: The app directory contains the manifest, package files, and generated source.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Product, category, template, or filesystem errors prevent project generation.

primary_path.action: Edit `manifest.yml` and change the issue-panel title to `Forge app for <your name>`.
details: The title becomes the panel heading and includes the development suffix before production.
primary_path.success_signal: The manifest saves with the tutorial's title change.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid YAML or module configuration causes the later deploy to report an error.

primary_path.action: Run `forge deploy` from the app's top-level directory and wait for build, compilation, and deployment.
primary_path.output: App deployed to the development environment.
primary_path.success_signal: Forge reports a successful deploy without compilation errors.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Lint, manifest, compile, bundle, authentication, or platform errors must be corrected before redeploying.

primary_path.action: Run `forge install`, select Jira as the Atlassian context, enter the developer-site URL, and complete any prompted consent.
details: Installation links the deployed app to the selected site with its required API access.
primary_path.input: Successful deploy and Jira development site URL.
primary_path.output: Forge app installed on the Jira site.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI displays the successful installation message.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong context, URL, permissions, consent, or installation error prevents the app from being ready to use.

primary_path.action: Create a Jira issue, open it, select the Apps grid button, and select the installed app.
details: The selected Forge module renders as an issue panel.
primary_path.input: Signed-in Jira user and installed Forge app.
primary_path.success_signal: The app panel is visible on the Jira issue with the configured title and hello-world content.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Anonymous access, missing install, wrong site, or an unavailable app entry means the candidate has not reached its documented terminal.

branches.path: Install nvm using its documented method, use nvm to install a supported Node.js LTS release, restart the terminal if necessary, and confirm `node --version` reports 22.x or 24.x before installing Forge CLI.
branches.effect_on_first_success: A Node version-manager setup precedes the CLI install and avoids mixing an unsupported runtime into the Forge path.

branches.condition: Node or Forge CLI installation reports permission errors or the new command is not found.
branches.path: Use Atlassian's linked Node installation and npm permission guidance, then restart the terminal and rerun `node --version`, the Forge CLI install, and `forge --version`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Installation recovery adds local environment repair and a terminal restart before authentication.

branches.condition: The local system lacks a supported keychain or libsecret.
branches.path: Set `FORGE_EMAIL` and `FORGE_API_TOKEN` as environment variables and do not run `forge login`; authenticated commands read those variables.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The browser and keychain login pages are replaced by environment configuration.

branches.condition: One of the four Forge product tutorials is selected.
branches.path: Run `forge create`, choose the tutorial's product and template, edit or use its hello-world code, deploy to the development environment, install on the selected site, grant any prompted consent, navigate to the product-specific module location, and verify its hello-world text. Use `forge tunnel` only where the tutorial places local iteration before the boundary.
branches.effect_on_first_success: A product-specific installed Forge module becomes visible in its Atlassian host.

branches.condition: An existing Connect app is being extended rather than a new cloud app created.
branches.path: Use Atlassian's incremental Connect-to-Forge adoption material instead of a new hello-world tutorial.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The app, permissions, deployment, and success boundary are migration-specific.

friction_gates.description: Supported Node.js LTS installation and `node --version` verification must precede npm-based Forge CLI installation and `forge --version` verification.
A scoped Forge API token with expiry and appropriate scopes must be created, copied, and used for CLI authentication.
Keychain and libsecret availability determines whether `forge login` or environment variables are used; root execution is prohibited.
A cloud developer site must be created and become ready before its setup wizard can complete.
Bitbucket, Confluence, Jira, or Jira Service Management must be selected for one visible first app.

sources.title: Prepare to build your first Forge app
sections_used: Log in with an Atlassian API scoped token

sources.title: Get started building Compass apps
sections_used: Get started with the Forge platform for Compass
evidence_supported: Product-specific template, deploy, install, and visible hello-world result

sources.title: Forge for Jira Software Cloud
evidence_supported: Forge recommendation for new Jira and Confluence cloud apps

sources.title: Build a Jira hello world app
sections_used: Set up a cloud developer site

uncertainties.question: Which Atlassian host product should define the first visible Forge app?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes product entitlement, template, manifest module, installation prompts, navigation, and completion signal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official shared setup guide explicitly routes to multiple product-specific hello worlds and does not select one.

excluded_after_success: No common after-success scope is selected while the host product is unresolved.
Marketplace listing, production environments, paid usage, permissions upgrades, storage, remotes, migrations, observability, and continuous delivery are excluded.
